warnings: PET degrades with repeated heat exposure
Not suitable for food-contact reuse without certified processing
Structural integrity decreases significantly after contamination
PET.notes: Common in bottles and food packaging. Moderate candidate for decorative or non-structural reuse.

warnings: Not suitable for food-contact reuse without certified processing
Structural reliability not guaranteed for load-bearing applications
HDPE.notes: One of the more suitable plastics for simple reuse scenarios. Found in detergent bottles, milk jugs, and caps.

warnings: PVC releases toxic chlorine compounds when heated or burned
Not recommended for any heat-based reuse process
Requires specialized handling and disposal
Not suitable for child-safe applications
Avoid mixing with other plastics in reuse streams
PVC.notes: High caution material. Generally not recommended for reuse in most scenarios due to chemical hazards.

warnings: Low structural strength limits load-bearing applications
Melts at relatively low temperatures
Not suitable for structural or safety-critical reuse
LDPE.notes: Flexible plastic found in bags and films. Limited structural value but can be pressed into sheets.

warnings: Not suitable for food-contact reuse without certified processing
Structural reliability not guaranteed for load-bearing applications
PP.notes: Decent candidate for lightweight, non-critical reuse. Common in caps, containers, and packaging.

warnings: PS is brittle and fractures easily under stress
Releases styrene vapors when heated — requires ventilation
Not suitable for structural applications
Not recommended for child-safe applications
Expanded PS (foam) has very limited reuse value
PS.notes: Brittle material with limited reuse potential. High caution for any heat-based processing.

warnings: Mixed or unidentified plastic composition increases processing risk
Unknown chemical additives may be present
Not suitable for food-contact or child-safe applications
Blending with known plastics not recommended without testing
OTHER.notes: Category 7 or unclassified plastics. Conservative scoring applies due to unknown composition.

warnings: Plastic type is unknown — all assessments are highly conservative
Do not apply heat without material identification
Unknown polymer composition significantly increases uncertainty
Not suitable for any safety-critical application
Material identification recommended before any reuse attempt
UNKNOWN.notes: Unknown plastic type. Identification is strongly recommended before any reuse decision.
